leadText: Eterna City (Japanese: ハクタイシティ Hakutai City) is a city in the Sinnoh region; it is an older city, showing the signs of history. There is a rather large statue of an ancient Pokémon (in Diamond and Brilliant Diamond, it is named as Dialga; in Pearl and Shining Pearl, Palkia; and in Platinum, it is a combination of both) on the east side of town. The statue was once the main center of festivals in the town, although signs of the town's culture and rituals have started to slip away. During the events of the games, both Team Galactic and Cynthia become interested in the statue. The city is home to the Underground Man, who teaches the player about the Underground/Grand Underground. Professor Oak also has a house in the southernmost part of the city, where he usually resides while he's staying in Sinnoh. In Platinum, after the player clears out the Team Galactic Eterna Building in the city and approaches the Bicycle Shop, Cynthia will appear and give them an Egg which will hatch into a Togepi.

sections.title: Slogan
sections.text: Slogan [ edit | edit source ] History Living (Japanese: むかしを いまにつなぐ まち The town that ties the past to now. )

sections.title: Places of interest
sections.text: Contents 1 Places of interest 1.1 Eterna Condominiums 1.2 Eterna Gym 1.3 Team Galactic Eterna Building 1.4 Herb Shop 1.5 Rad Rickshaw's Cycle Shop 1.6 Ancient statue Places of interest [ edit | edit source ] Eterna Condominiums [ edit | edit source ] Main article: Eterna Condominiums Located east of the Poké Mart, Sinnoh's Name Rater can be found here, as well as a person named Norton who will trade a Chatot for a Buizel , and another person who will give away TM67 ( Recycle ). Eterna Gym [ edit | edit source ] Main article: Eterna Gym Located in the southeast of the city, the Eterna Gym is run by the Gym Leader Gardenia , who specializes in Grass-type Pokémon. Defeating Gardenia yields the Forest Badge , TM86 ( Grass Knot ), and the ability to use Cut outside of battle. Team Galactic Eterna Building [ edit | edit source ] Main article: Team Galactic Eterna Building Located in the northwest of the city, this is one of the two Team Galactic headquarters. In Pokémon Platinum , the building looks vastly different from its appearance in Pokémon Diamond and Pearl . To reach the building, the move Cut must be used on a small tree blocking the entrance. Herb Shop [ edit | edit source ] Main article: Herb Shop The Herb Shop is located to the east of the Team Galactic Eterna Building, and sells effective yet inexpensive herbs. Herb Shop Heal Powder $ 450 Energy Powder $ 500 Energy Root $ 800 Revival Herb $ 2,800 Rad Rickshaw's Cycle Shop [ edit | edit source ] Main article: Rad Rickshaw's Cycle Shop This Bicycle shop is located just southeast of the Pokémon Center. The player cannot purchase a Bicycle here, but one is instead obtained for free once the store's owner is rescued from the Team Galactic Eterna Building. Ancient statue [ edit | edit source ] The statue in Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl This Pokémon statue is up a little hill to the east of Eterna City. In Diamond and Pearl and Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl , the statue has a plate (dependent on the game), while in Pokémon Platinum the plate has been torn off the statue. It was once the centerpiece of many festivals. A man in the house just south of the statue will tell the player the two inscriptions. In the Team Galactic HQ building in Veilstone City , a report can be found with missing text generated with a machine (indicated by "...") when described by the man in the house. The text extracted by the machine is written in bolded text. The first one says this: The creation of DIA LGA , The giver of time... In laughter, there is tears... And, likewise it is with time. The same time flows on. For it is the blessing of DIA LGA . The second one says this: The birth of PAL KIA . The creator of parallel dimensions... Alive, yet not alive... Everything d rifts in space... To arrive in the same univer se. It is the blessing of PAL KIA .
